name: Stages
description: Six-segment generator. Each segment configurable as RAMP · STEP · HOLD via TYPE button. Chained segments form envelopes, LFOs, sequencers, or complex multi-stage shapes. Per-segment audio oscillator mode via long-press (1.2 firmware).

controls.name: Segment Sliders (×6)
controls.description: Main level/time control per segment. RAMP: duration. STEP: target voltage. HOLD: voltage level held for the SHAPE/TIME duration. Full-size 60mm sliders.
controls.detail: Slider LED color shows segment TYPE (green/orange/red). Blinks on activity.

controls.name: SHAPE / TIME Pot (×6)
controls.description: Sub-knob per segment. RAMP: shape curve (expo→lin→log). STEP: glide time to target. HOLD: hold duration. Accepts CV via back-panel expander.
controls.detail: CCW: expo/short · noon: linear · CW: log/long.

controls.name: TYPE Button (×6)
controls.description: Cycles segment type: RAMP (green) → STEP (orange) → HOLD (red). Long-press 1s = toggle loop on that segment (LED blinks). Long-press 3s on a solo segment = audio oscillator mode.
controls.detail: In color-blind mode (hold left button at boot): brightness/blink patterns replace color cues.

controls.name: Segment Grouping via GATE Jacks
controls.description: Patching a cable into a GATE jack marks the start of a new group. All unpatched GATE segments to its right join that group until the next patched GATE. This is how chains are defined.
controls.detail: Small LEDs between sliders show group boundaries.

inputs.name: GATE (×6)
inputs.description: Per-segment gate/trigger input. Patching a GATE jack starts a new group at that segment. RAMP/HOLD: advance on rising edge. STEP: glides to target, waits for trigger before advancing.

outputs.name: OUT (×6)
outputs.description: Per-segment output. In a group, only the last segment's OUT is typically used as the group's output; earlier OUTs reflect the individual segment's stage.

behaviors.name: Group definition by GATE patching
behaviors.description: Inserting a jack in any GATE input marks the start of a group. Unpatched GATEs to its right join the group. Build envelopes, LFOs, or sequencers of any length 1–6 by placing GATE cables to define group boundaries.

behaviors.name: RAMP segment (green)
behaviors.description: Output smoothly ramps from the previous voltage to the slider's level over the duration set by the segment. SHAPE pot selects curve (expo/lin/log).

behaviors.name: STEP segment (orange)
behaviors.description: Output glides to the slider's target voltage (glide time set by SHAPE pot), then holds there until the next trigger arrives. This is the sequencer-step mode.

behaviors.name: HOLD segment (red)
behaviors.description: Output stays at the slider's voltage for a duration set by the SHAPE pot, then hands off to the next segment. Acts as a sustain plateau in envelopes or a fixed-length step in sequences.

behaviors.name: Loop mode (long-press TYPE 1s)
behaviors.description: Makes a segment loop on itself — LED blinks to confirm. To loop a group, long-press the TYPE buttons of the first and last segments of the group simultaneously.

behaviors.name: Per-segment audio oscillator (1.2 firmware)
behaviors.trigger: hold TYPE button ≥3s on a solo segment
behaviors.description: Converts a solo segment into a band-limited audio oscillator, ±5V. Slider = pitch (C4 mid-slider), SHAPE pot morphs through variable-slope triangle → saw → saw/square blend → variable-width pulse. Slider LED blink slows near C notes for tuning.

behaviors.name: Extended sequencer (1.2 firmware)
behaviors.trigger: place HOLD or RAMP segment at start of a clocked group
behaviors.description: The leading segment becomes a control segment (not part of the sequence). Its SHAPE pot picks playback mode (Up/Down/Pendulum/Alternating/Random/Pseudo-random/Addressable). RAMP control segments quantize the 0–1V range in semitones; HOLD uses full 0–8V unquantized.

behaviors.name: Multi-module chain
behaviors.description: Daisy-chain up to six Stages units via the provided 3-pin cables to extend groups across modules — segments span physical units as one long chain.

behaviors.name: Color-blind mode
behaviors.trigger: hold the leftmost TYPE button during power-on
behaviors.description: Replaces RAMP/STEP/HOLD color differentiation with brightness and blinking patterns for better contrast.

patchIdeas.name: Six-step CV sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: All six segments set to STEP so each slider is a target voltage; clock into GATE 1 advances the chain step-by-step.
steps: Press TYPE on each of segments 1–6 until the LED is orange (STEP).
Set all six sliders to the step voltages you want (use the slider LED ladder as a pitch ruler).
Turn every SHAPE/TIME pot fully CCW — shortest glide, so steps jump cleanly.
Patch your clock (Out) into GATE 1 only. Leave GATE 2–6 unpatched so segments 2–6 join the group.
Patch OUT 6 into a quantizer's In, then quantizer Out into a VCO's V/Oct.
Mult the clock to a separate envelope trigger so each step articulates with an AD envelope on a VCA.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stages OUT 6 — stepped 0–8V sequence, one step per clock pulse.
patchIdeas.hear: A six-note CV lane that advances on every clock. Raising the SHAPE/TIME pot on any step adds portamento into that note; dropping a slider rewrites the scale-degree on the fly.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* All segments: STEP (orange)
* All SHAPE/TIME: fully CCW (fast glide)

- Clock (Out) c> Stages (GATE 1)
- Stages (OUT 6) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Clock (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Full ADSR envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Four-segment chain — RAMP attack, RAMP decay, HOLD sustain, RAMP release — triggered from one keyboard gate on GATE 1.
steps: Set segment 1 to RAMP (green): slider up = peak level, SHAPE/TIME sets attack curve (noon = linear).
Set segment 2 to RAMP (green): slider at the sustain level, SHAPE/TIME sets decay curve.
Set segment 3 to HOLD (red): slider matches segment 2's slider (the sustain plateau). SHAPE/TIME = maximum CW so the hold lasts as long as the gate.
Set segment 4 to RAMP (green): slider at 0V (fully down), SHAPE/TIME sets release curve.
Patch your keyboard Gate into GATE 1. Leave GATE 2–4 unpatched so all four segments form one group.
Patch OUT 4 into your VCA's CV input.
patchIdeas.hear: Classic ADSR response to keypresses. Sculpt the attack/decay/release curves with each segment's SHAPE/TIME pot — expo for pluck, log for pad swells.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1: RAMP (attack)
* Seg 2: RAMP (decay)
* Seg 3: HOLD (sustain)
* Seg 4: RAMP (release)

- Keyboard (Gate) g> Stages (GATE 1)
- Stages (OUT 4) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Complex-shape free-running LFO
patchIdeas.patch: Six RAMP segments looped on themselves produce a six-vertex non-sinusoidal LFO — no clock needed.
steps: Press TYPE on each of segments 1–6 until green (RAMP).
Alternate slider heights — e.g. high/low/mid/high/low/mid — to shape the waveform's vertices.
Set SHAPE/TIME on each segment to taste (CCW = expo curve, CW = log curve, noon = linear).
Long-press TYPE on segment 1 AND segment 6 at the same time — the segment 1 LED blinks, confirming the whole group loops.
Leave all GATE jacks unpatched so the six segments form one group.
Patch OUT 6 into your filter's cutoff CV input.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stages OUT 6 — free-running 0–8V LFO with a six-vertex shape.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow asymmetric modulation that never sounds like a sine or triangle. Each slider is a waypoint; each SHAPE/TIME pot is the curve getting there. Move one slider live to reshape the LFO in real time.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* All segments: RAMP (green)
* Long-press TYPE on seg 1 + seg 6 together to loop the group

- Stages (OUT 6) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Two independent AD envelopes
patchIdeas.patch: Split the six segments into two three-segment AD envelopes, each triggered by its own gate.
steps: Segments 1–3: set seg 1 RAMP (attack), seg 2 HOLD (brief plateau), seg 3 RAMP (decay to 0V).
Segments 4–6: same pattern — seg 4 RAMP (attack), seg 5 HOLD, seg 6 RAMP (decay to 0V).
Patch your Gate A into GATE 1 — this starts group A on segments 1–3.
Patch your Gate B into GATE 4 — this starts group B on segments 4–6 (the patched cable is what splits the groups).
Leave GATE 2, 3, 5, 6 unpatched.
Patch OUT 3 → VCA 1 CV. Patch OUT 6 → VCA 2 CV.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stages OUT 3 and OUT 6 — two independent 0–8V AD envelopes.
patchIdeas.hear: Two separate percussive envelopes from one module. Fire them from different triggers for layered drum voices or a dual-VCA ducking setup.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1-3: RAMP/HOLD/RAMP (env A)
* Seg 4-6: RAMP/HOLD/RAMP (env B)

- Gate A (Out) g> Stages (GATE 1)
- Gate B (Out) g> Stages (GATE 4)
- Stages (OUT 3) >> VCA 1 (CV)
- Stages (OUT 6) >> VCA 2 (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Slew / portamento limiter
patchIdeas.patch: A single STEP segment with a short glide smooths incoming pitch CV — slider sets the target range, SHAPE/TIME sets glide time.
steps: Set segment 1 to STEP (orange).
Push segment 1's slider fully up so incoming pitch CV isn't clipped.
Set SHAPE/TIME on segment 1 to about 10 o'clock for a short glide; rotate CW for longer portamento.
Patch your sequencer's gate (or note-trigger) into GATE 1 — this advances the STEP each note.
Patch the same sequencer's pitch CV into the back-panel SLIDER CV input for segment 1 (requires expander) OR use the normal-level-tracking trick: if no expander, see the alternate slew patch below.
Patch OUT 1 into the VCO V/Oct.
Alternate without expander: use a RAMP segment with SHAPE/TIME as glide, driving GATE 1 every note-change — gives asymmetric slew limiter behavior.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stages OUT 1 — smoothed pitch CV with portamento between each new note.
patchIdeas.hear: Pitch glides into each new sequencer note instead of snapping — synth-lead expressiveness. CW SHAPE/TIME = longer glide, CCW = sharper staircase.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1: STEP (orange) · SHAPE ~10 o'clock

- Sequencer (Gate) g> Stages (GATE 1)
- Sequencer (Pitch CV) >> Stages (SLIDER CV 1 via expander)
- Stages (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Audio oscillator + envelope on one module
patchIdeas.patch: Segment 1 long-pressed 3s becomes a pitched audio oscillator (1.2 firmware); segments 3–6 form an ADSR fired by the same gate that plays notes.
steps: Patch your V/Oct pitch CV into GATE 1 (note: in audio oscillator mode segment 1 reads its pitch from the SLIDER CV input via expander, or from its slider if no expander).
Hold segment 1's TYPE button for 3+ seconds — LED goes into the audio-oscillator blink pattern (complex sequence).
Set segment 1's slider near the middle (C4 = mid-slider).
Rotate segment 1 SHAPE/TIME through its range: fully CCW = triangle, noon = saw, CW = saw/square blend, fully CW = pulse. Pick a waveform.
Set segments 3–6 as RAMP/RAMP/HOLD/RAMP (attack/decay/sustain/release).
Patch your keyboard Gate into GATE 3 — this starts the envelope group on segments 3–6.
Patch OUT 1 → VCA audio in.
Patch OUT 6 → VCA CV.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stages OUT 1 — ±5V pitched audio; Stages OUT 6 — 0–8V ADSR.
patchIdeas.hear: A complete mono voice from one Stages: the oscillator sings, the envelope shapes it. Slider LED blink rate slows near C notes as a tuning aid.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1: Audio oscillator (long-press TYPE 3s)
* Seg 3-6: RAMP/RAMP/HOLD/RAMP (ADSR group)

- Keyboard (Gate) g> Stages (GATE 3)
- Stages (OUT 1) -> VCA (In)
- Stages (OUT 6) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Looping envelope = tempo-less LFO
patchIdeas.patch: A three-segment AD envelope looped on itself becomes a controllable asymmetric LFO with attack, peak, and decay shapes you can tune live.
steps: Segments 1–3: seg 1 RAMP (rise), seg 2 HOLD (peak dwell), seg 3 RAMP (fall to 0V).
Long-press TYPE on segment 1 AND segment 3 simultaneously — segment 1's LED starts blinking, confirming the group loops.
Seg 1 slider = peak height. Seg 1 SHAPE/TIME = rise time. Seg 2 slider = peak height (match seg 1). Seg 2 SHAPE/TIME = dwell time. Seg 3 slider = 0V. Seg 3 SHAPE/TIME = fall time.
Leave GATE 1–3 unpatched — looping handles self-retrigger.
Patch OUT 3 to your filter cutoff or VCA CV.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stages OUT 3 — looping 0–8V envelope that cycles continuously.
patchIdeas.hear: A tempo-less LFO with independent control over rise shape, peak dwell, and fall shape. Pull seg 2's SHAPE/TIME CCW to get a pure triangle LFO; pull it CW to hold the peak before falling.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1: RAMP (rise)
* Seg 2: HOLD (peak dwell)
* Seg 3: RAMP (fall)
* Long-press TYPE on seg 1 + seg 3 to loop the group

- Stages (OUT 3) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Sequencer + envelope combo
patchIdeas.patch: Segments 1–3 are a three-step STEP sequencer, segments 4–6 are an AD envelope. Clock drives both.
steps: Segments 1–3: set each to STEP (orange). Sliders = the three step pitches. SHAPE/TIME ≈ CCW for clean jumps.
Segments 4–6: seg 4 RAMP (attack), seg 5 HOLD (brief plateau), seg 6 RAMP (decay).
Patch your clock into GATE 1 — starts the sequencer group.
Patch the same clock (multed) into GATE 4 — starts the envelope group and fires it on every step.
Patch OUT 3 → Quantizer → VCO V/Oct.
Patch OUT 6 → VCA CV.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stages OUT 3 — stepped pitch CV; Stages OUT 6 — 0–8V AD envelope, one hit per clock.
patchIdeas.hear: A three-note loop playing with percussive articulation from the same clock. Tweak seg 6's SHAPE/TIME to stretch or shorten the decay under every note.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1-3: STEP (sequencer)
* Seg 4-6: RAMP/HOLD/RAMP (envelope)

- Clock (Out) c> Stages (GATE 1)
- Clock (Out) c> Stages (GATE 4)
- Stages (OUT 3) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Stages (OUT 6) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Extended sequencer with pendulum playback (1.2)
patchIdeas.patch: 1.2-firmware feature: a HOLD 'control segment' at the start of a clocked group picks the playback mode for the remaining STEP segments.
steps: Set segment 1 to HOLD (red) — this becomes the control segment; its SHAPE/TIME now picks playback mode, not hold duration.
Set segments 2–6 to STEP (orange) — these are the five actual sequencer steps.
Rotate segment 1's SHAPE/TIME to about 2 o'clock for Pendulum mode (order: CCW→CW the pot walks Up, Down, Pendulum, Alternating, Random, Pseudo-random, Addressable).
Set the five step sliders (segments 2–6) to the step voltages.
Patch your clock into GATE 1 — the control segment reads it and drives the five steps in pendulum order.
Patch OUT 6 → VCO V/Oct (if segment 1 is RAMP instead of HOLD, the output is pre-quantized to semitones across 0–1V).
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stages OUT 6 — stepped CV playing segments 2–6 in pendulum (1→2→3→4→5→4→3→2→1) order.
patchIdeas.hear: A back-and-forth sequence without a dedicated sequencer. Switch playback modes live by rotating segment 1's SHAPE/TIME — random pot position produces a sample-and-hold-like scramble.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1: HOLD = control segment · SHAPE ~2 o'clock (Pendulum)
* Seg 2-6: STEP (sequencer steps)

- Clock (Out) c> Stages (GATE 1)
- Stages (OUT 6) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Multi-module chain — 12-step sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: Link two Stages via the back-panel ribbon cable; the two 6-segment groups act as one 12-step chain.
steps: Power both modules OFF. Connect the 3-pin ribbon cable between the two Stages' back-panel expansion headers.
Power up — the two modules now share chain logic.
On Stages A, set all six segments to STEP (orange). On Stages B, same — all STEP.
Patch your clock into Stages A GATE 1 only. Leave every other GATE unpatched on both modules so all 12 segments join one group.
Set all 12 sliders as your 12-step pitch sequence.
Patch Stages B OUT 6 (the final segment) → Quantizer → VCO V/Oct.
Patch the clock (multed) → envelope trigger → VCA CV for articulation.
patchIdeas.hear: A single twelve-step loop spread across two physical modules. The ribbon cable makes them behave as one module — you can extend up to six chained Stages units for a 36-step chain.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages A + Stages B (3-pin ribbon linked):
* All 12 segments: STEP (orange)
* Clock patched only on Stages A GATE 1

- Clock (Out) c> Stages A (GATE 1)
- Stages B (OUT 6) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Clock (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
